pg44-p2-s1: In the preceding chapters you have learned something of alcoholism.
pg44-p2-s2:
We hope we have made clear the distinction between
the alcoholic and the nonalcoholic.
pg44-p2-s3:
If, when you honestly want to, you find you cannot quit entirely, or if when
drinking, you have little control over the amount you take, you are probably alcoholic.
pg44-p2-s4:
If that be the case, you may be
suffering from an illness which only a spiritual experience will conquer.
pg44-p2:
In the preceding chapters you have learned something of alcoholism. We hope we have made clear the distinction between
the alcoholic and the nonalcoholic. If, when you honestly want to, you find you cannot quit entirely, or if when
drinking, you have little control over the amount you take, you are probably alcoholic. If that be the case, you may be
suffering from an illness which only a spiritual experience will conquer.
pg44-p3-s1:
To one who feels he is an atheist or agnostic such an experience seems impossible, but to continue as he is means
disaster, especially if he is an alcoholic of the hopeless variety.
pg44-p3-s2:
To be doomed to an alcoholic death or to live on a
spiritual basis are not always easy alternatives to face.
pg44-p3:
To one who feels he is an atheist or agnostic such an experience seems impossible, but to continue as he is means
disaster, especially if he is an alcoholic of the hopeless variety. To be doomed to an alcoholic death or to live on a
spiritual basis are not always easy alternatives to face.
pg44-p4-s2: About half our original fellowship were of exactly that type.
pg44-p4-s3:
At first some of us tried to
avoid the issue, hoping against hope we were not true alcoholics.
pg44-p4-s4:
But after a while we had to face the fact that we must
find a spiritual basis of life - or else.
pg44-p4-s5: Perhaps it is going to be that way with you.
pg44-p4-s6:
But cheer up, something like half
of us thought we were atheists or agnostics.
pg44-p4-s7: Our experience shows that you need not be disconcerted.
pg44-p4:
But it isn't so difficult. About half our original fellowship were of exactly that type. At first some of us tried to
avoid the issue, hoping against hope we were not true alcoholics. But after a while we had to face the fact that we must
find a spiritual basis of life - or else. Perhaps it is going to be that way with you. But cheer up, something like half
of us thought we were atheists or agnostics. Our experience shows that you need not be disconcerted.
pg44-p5-s1: If a mere code of morals or a better philosophy of life were sufficient to overcome alcoholism, many of us
